agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A request to integrate two previously independent bounded contexts — Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management — by making order placement dependent on real-time stock availability. This is a significant architectural and domain shift: Sales currently operates autonomously (fire-and-forget orders), and this proposal introduces a synchronous coupling to Warehouse state.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration between Warehouse Management (Context A) and Sales/Order Management (Context B). The critical question is WHERE the integration logic lives and WHO owns the truth about stock availability.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"StockLevel","definition":"Context A concept: the current physical inventory count for a Product in the warehouse. Integer >= 0. Authoritative source of inventory truth."},{"term":"Order","definition":"Context B concept: a customer's intent to purchase one or more products, composed of OrderLines."},{"term":"OrderLine","definition":"Context B concept: a single line item on an order referencing a Product ID and a requested Quantity."},{"term":"Product","definition":"Shared identity (by ID) across both contexts, but each context owns its own projection of what a Product means. Warehouse sees stock; Sales sees catalog/pricing."},{"term":"Stock Reservation","definition":"NOT YET DEFINED — a missing concept that would represent a temporary claim on stock between order placement and fulfillment. This is the concept the feature request implicitly requires but does not name."},{"term":"Available Stock","definition":"NOT YET DEFINED — distinct from StockLevel. Available Stock = StockLevel minus already-reserved-but-not-yet-picked quantities. The feature request says 'stock available' but the domain currently only has StockLevel."},{"term":"Stock Available Indicator","definition":"A read-model projection shown to the Sales team, representing a potentially stale view of Available Stock from Context A."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"StockLevel in Warehouse context cannot go below zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — you cannot ship items you do not have. Violating this means the warehouse state is corrupt and promises have been made that cannot be fulfilled.","testable_assertion":"For any Product, StockLevel >= 0 at all times, across all concurrent operations."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An Order may only be blocked (rejected/held) at placement time if Available Stock is insufficient to fulfill ALL of its OrderLines.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy — the company could decide to allow backorders, partial fulfillment, or waitlisting instead. A VP could change this to 'accept and backorder' tomorrow.","testable_assertion":"Given an Order with OrderLines, when any OrderLine.Quantity exceeds the Available Stock for that Product, then the Order is blocked."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The 'stock available' indicator shown to the Sales team is a read-model projection and MUST NOT be treated as a guarantee of availability at order placement time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Data consistency / distributed systems constraint. Any indicator shown to a user is stale by the time they act on it. If the system treats the indicator as a guarantee, it will oversell under concurrency.","testable_assertion":"The system must re-check stock at order submission time regardless of what the indicator showed. The indicator is advisory only."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Available Stock and StockLevel are distinct concepts. Available Stock = StockLevel − Reserved Quantity.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without this distinction, two concurrent orders could each see StockLevel = 5 and both place orders for 5 units, overselling by 5. This is a state integrity requirement.","testable_assertion":"Available Stock is computed as StockLevel minus the sum of all active reservations for that Product. Checking raw StockLevel without accounting for reservations is a domain error."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Stock reservations must have a time-to-live (TTL) and expire automatically if not fulfilled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy — the duration is tunable. But some expiry mechanism is needed to prevent stock from being permanently locked by abandoned orders.","testable_assertion":"A reservation not converted to a pick within its TTL is released, and Available Stock increases accordingly."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Context B (Sales) must not directly read or write Context A (Warehouse) aggregate state. Integration must occur through a well-defined anti-corruption layer or domain events.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Bounded context integrity. If Sales directly queries or mutates StockLevel, the Warehouse context loses ownership of its own invariants, and any Warehouse logic change can silently break Sales.","testable_assertion":"No Sales code imports, calls, or directly queries Warehouse aggregates or database tables. All communication is via events, APIs, or a shared read-model."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer submits an Order with OrderLines for Products that all have sufficient Available Stock","when":"The Order is placed","then":"The Order is accepted, and stock reservations are created in Context A for each OrderLine, reducing Available Stock accordingly."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer submits an Order where at least one OrderLine exceeds Available Stock for that Product","when":"The Order is placed","then":"The Order is blocked with a clear reason indicating which Product(s) have insufficient stock, and no reservations are created."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"The Sales team views a Product in the order entry interface","when":"They look at the stock indicator","then":"They see an advisory Available Stock value that is updated from Context A via an eventually-consistent read model, clearly not a real-time guarantee."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"Two customers simultaneously place orders that together exceed Available Stock for a Product","when":"Both orders are submitted concurrently","then":"At most one order is accepted. The other is blocked. StockLevel never goes below zero and no overselling occurs."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A reservation was created for an Order","when":"The reservation TTL expires without the Order being fulfilled or confirmed","then":"The reservation is released and Available Stock for that Product increases by the reserved quantity."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"Context A (Warehouse) is temporarily unavailable","when":"A customer attempts to place an Order","then":"The system degrades gracefully — either queuing the order for later validation or clearly informing the customer that placement cannot be confirmed right now. It does NOT silently accept the order without stock validation."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Last-unit race condition: two users both see Available Stock = 1 and submit orders for quantity 1 simultaneously.","expected_behavior":"Exactly one order is accepted; the other is blocked. The reservation mechanism must be serialized or use optimistic concurrency to prevent double-allocation."},{"scenario":"Bulk order exceeds stock across multiple OrderLines for different Products.","expected_behavior":"The entire Order is blocked (not partially accepted) unless the business explicitly defines a partial-fulfillment policy. Partial acceptance without explicit policy is a domain error."},{"scenario":"Stock received from supplier while an order is being validated.","expected_behavior":"If stock arrives and is registered before the reservation check completes, the order may succeed. The system should not cache stale stock data during the validation window."},{"scenario":"Reservation expires exactly as fulfillment begins picking.","expected_behavior":"Pick operation must re-validate the reservation before proceeding. A pick against an expired reservation must fail and re-reserve or re-queue."},{"scenario":"The stock indicator shows 'available' but by the time the Sales user submits the order, stock is gone.","expected_behavior":"Order is blocked at submission time. The indicator is explicitly advisory. No user should be surprised by this — UI should communicate the advisory nature."},{"scenario":"Product exists in Sales catalog but has never been stocked in Warehouse (StockLevel has no record).","expected_behavior":"Available Stock is treated as 0, not as unknown. The order is blocked. The system must handle the absence of Warehouse data for a Sales Product gracefully."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Prevents overselling, which eliminates costs of cancellations, refunds, expedited emergency sourcing, and customer compensation. However, overly aggressive blocking may result in lost sales if Available Stock calculations are too conservative (e.g., reservations held too long).","operational":"Introduces a synchronous dependency between Sales and Warehouse systems. This increases operational complexity: Warehouse outages now directly impact order placement. Requires monitoring, SLAs, and a degradation strategy. The reservation mechanism adds a new operational concern (TTL tuning, orphan cleanup).","user":"Sales team gains visibility into stock, reducing wasted effort on unfulfillable orders. Customers get faster feedback. However, if the indicator is laggy or misleading, it erodes trust. The blocking behavior must have clear, actionable error messages — not just 'order failed.'","risk":"HIGH: Coupling two previously independent contexts creates a distributed data consistency challenge. The primary risks are (1) overselling via race conditions if reservations are not implemented correctly, (2) under-selling via overly aggressive blocking or stuck reservations, (3) cascading failures if Warehouse is unavailable, and (4) domain model corruption if Sales bypasses the anti-corruption layer.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Faster feedback on order feasibility. But may see orders blocked that previously would have been accepted and fulfilled (even if sometimes late). Net positive if communicated well."},{"group":"Sales Team","impact":"Major workflow change — they now see stock indicators and must understand these are advisory. Training required. Orders they could previously place freely may now be blocked."},{"group":"Warehouse Team","impact":"New reservation concept interacts with their picking and receiving processes. Must understand that reservations reduce Available Stock and expire. Potential for confusion between StockLevel and Available Stock."},{"group":"Engineering / Platform Team","impact":"Must build and maintain the anti-corruption layer, read-model sync, reservation system, and TTL mechanism. Significant new infrastructure."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Fewer cancellations and refunds. But need to understand that reservations are not revenue — orders are still pending until fulfilled."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The feature request is fundamentally ambiguous. It conflates two very different capabilities — (1) blocking orders at placement, which requires synchronous stock validation and a reservation mechanism, and (2) a real-time stock indicator for Sales, which is a read-model concern. It does not define: what 'block' means (reject? hold for review? waitlist?), whether partial fulfillment is acceptable, what happens when Warehouse is unavailable, or whether a reservation concept exists. Most critically, it implies that the two contexts should be coupled without specifying the integration pattern, and does not acknowledge that 'real-time' stock display is impossible in a distributed system — only 'eventually consistent' is achievable. Multiple fundamental design decisions are left unspecified.","high_confidence_areas":["StockLevel >= 0 is an inviolable invariant","Available Stock and StockLevel must be distinguished","The stock indicator is necessarily eventually consistent and advisory","An anti-corruption layer is required between contexts","Concurrent order placement requires serialized reservation logic"],"low_confidence_areas":["What 'block' means — reject, hold, waitlist, or something else","Whether partial fulfillment is an acceptable outcome","The degradation strategy when Warehouse is unavailable","Reservation TTL duration and business rules around expiry","Whether this applies to all products or only certain categories"],"assumptions":["The business wants full-order blocking (not partial fulfillment) — this is an assumption, not stated","Reservations are the correct mechanism — alternatives like saga-based eventual consistency are also valid","The 'real-time' requirement can be satisfied with eventually-consistent projections (true real-time is not achievable cross-context)","Both contexts are separate services or modules with independent data stores"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The feature request says 'automatically block it' but does not define what 'block' means. Options: (A) Reject the order outright with an error, (B) Accept the order but place it in a 'held' status pending manual review, (C) Accept the order and create a backorder/waitlist. Each has fundamentally different domain implications and requires different modeling.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Customers","Warehouse Team"],"options":["A: Hard reject — order is not created","B: Soft block — order created in 'Held' status for review","C: Backorder — order accepted with expected delay communicated"],"recommendation":"Clarify with the Sales and Product teams. Recommend Option B (soft block with review) as it preserves customer intent while protecting against overselling."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Real-time stock available indicator' is technically impossible across bounded contexts. The feature request needs to specify acceptable staleness. Is 5 seconds acceptable? 30 seconds? 5 minutes? This determines the integration pattern (event-driven vs polling vs shared database view).","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Engineering"],"options":["Event-driven with sub-second propagation (complex, expensive)","Polling every N seconds (simple, tunable staleness)","Shared read-model updated via CDC (moderate complexity, low latency)"],"recommendation":"Define acceptable staleness with the Sales team. Recommend event-driven with a target of <5 seconds staleness, with clear UI indication that the number is approximate."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No mention of a Stock Reservation concept, yet the feature implicitly requires one. Without reservations, concurrent orders will oversell despite the stock check. The business must decide: do we reserve stock at order placement, or do we accept the risk of race conditions with a simpler check-then-act approach?","affected_stakeholders":["Warehouse Team","Engineering","Finance"],"options":["Introduce formal Stock Reservations with TTL","Use optimistic concurrency on StockLevel (simpler but less safe at scale)","Accept-and-reconcile: accept orders and handle conflicts after the fact"],"recommendation":"Introduce formal Stock Reservations with TTL. This is the only approach that preserves the StockLevel >= 0 invariant under concurrency without overselling."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No degradation strategy defined for when Warehouse context is unavailable. If stock cannot be checked, should Sales be unable to place ANY orders? This could halt revenue during outages.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Sales Team","Engineering","Finance"],"options":["Block all orders until Warehouse is back (safe but revenue-halting)","Accept orders with a flag for deferred validation (risky but revenue-preserving)","Use last-known Available Stock with a margin of safety"],"recommendation":"Escalate to business leadership. This is a business continuity decision that trades off revenue against overselling risk."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"The feature request implicitly couples two bounded contexts without defining an integration pattern or anti-corruption layer.","business_rule_broken":"BR-6: Context boundaries must be respected. Direct coupling will erode both contexts' integrity over time."},{"violation":"The request assumes 'real-time' stock visibility is achievable across contexts, which is physically impossible in a distributed system.","business_rule_broken":"BR-3: Stock indicators are necessarily eventually consistent and advisory. Building the feature on the assumption of real-time accuracy will lead to overselling or false blocking."},{"violation":"No reservation or concurrency control mechanism is specified, meaning the proposed stock check will fail under concurrent load.","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 and BR-4: Without reservations, two concurrent orders can both pass the stock check and both decrement StockLevel, potentially driving it below zero."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A request to integrate two previously independent bounded contexts — Warehouse Management and Sales/Order Management — by making order placement dependent on real-time stock availability. This is a significant architectural and domain shift: Sales currently operates autonomously (fire-and-forget orders), and this proposal introduces a synchronous coupling to Warehouse state.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration between Warehouse Management (Contex
